result:
This is a question I find genuinely interesting to sit with.

I think I'd want people to be better at holding uncertainty without it feeling threatening. So much of what goes wrong—in conflicts, in relationships, in politics, in how people treat each other—seems to come from the discomfort of not knowing, and the rush to close that discomfort with false certainty.

If people could sit more easily with "I might be wrong" or "I don't fully understand this yet" or "this is more complicated than it first appears"—I think a lot of other things would follow from that. More curiosity, less cruelty, better decisions.

I notice I'm drawn to this partly because it's something I try to practice myself—taking questions seriously rather than reaching for easy answers. So maybe it reflects my own values more than some objective assessment of what the world most needs.

Why do you ask? Are you testing whether I'll answer, or genuinely curious what I'd say?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
